title: Rule 1
plain: Notice of a new timetable/supplement is posted by operating bulletin at least 72 hours prior, and given by GBO at least 24 hours prior.

title: Rule 2
plain: Rule 2 (Watches): conductors, LEs, foremen, etc. must use a reliable watch indicating hours, minutes and seconds and keep it correct.

title: Rule 11
plain: Rule 11: a fusee should not be placed on a public crossing at grade or where it may cause fire.

title: Rule 12
plain: Doubt = STOP. The same principle applies on the radio (123.2(v)).

title: Rule 14
plain: Rule 14 (Engine Whistle Signals) prescribes the crossing whistle signal; 'engine whistle' also refers to 'engine horn.'

title: Rule 26
plain: Blue signal protection: workmen are about the equipment; it must not be coupled to or moved until those workmen remove the signal.

title: Rule 40
plain: Rule 40(a): special instructions specify when Rules 42/842, 43/843 and 849 are applicable on non-main track.

title: Rule 41
plain: Rule 41 is not applicable on main tracks outside of cautionary limits, signalled sidings and other signalled tracks, or tracks specified in special instructions.

title: Rule 42
plain: Rule 42(a): when protection is required, the request must be in writing and on the prescribed form.

title: Rule 43
plain: Rule 43: the request must be in writing (when practicable on the prescribed form); after GBO protection, the speed restriction(s) and limits are confirmed to the foreman in writing.

title: Rule 66
plain: Rule 66 — Securing Equipment After an Emergency Brake Application on Grade — adds requirements on top of the general Rule 112 securement.

title: Rule 103.1
plain: Rule 103.1(a): the minimum-required-time observation applies unless manual protection is provided at the crossing.

title: Rule 104
plain: Rule 104: hand-operated switches in sidings may be considered lined for the normal position unless advised otherwise — but verify, because 'considered' is not 'confirmed.'

title: Rule 104.1
plain: Rule 104.1(a): a spring switch is identified by a spring-switch sign bearing the letters 'SS'.

title: Rule 104.3
plain: Rule 104.3: a crew member must observe that the points are lined for the authorized route before the movement proceeds over a power-operated switch at a stop signal.

title: Rule 104.4
plain: Rule 104.4(a): a semi-automatic switch is equipped with reflectorized targets.

title: Rule 104.5
plain: Rule 104.5: a derail is switch-stand operated and identified by a reflective red letter 'D' on a reflective yellow target (or a 'Special Derail' sign), visible when in the derailing position.

title: Rule 105
plain: Non-main track: REDUCED speed, not exceeding 15 MPH unless signal indication or special instructions allow more.

title: Rule 106
plain: Rule 106: all crew members are responsible for the safe operation of movements/equipment in their charge and for the observance of the rules.

title: Rule 109
plain: Rule 109: removing the reverser (unless excepted) prevents the locomotive from being powered/moved while the engineer is away from the controls.

title: Rule 112
plain: Rule 112(ii): parking brakes are considered to be hand brakes.

title: Rule 113.0
plain: Ensure at least one knuckle is open before coupling — otherwise you get a missed (by-pass) coupling.

title: Rule 113.1
plain: Rule 113.1(a): equipment is considered uncoupled once the uncoupling lever has been lifted.

title: Rule 113.2
plain: Rule 113.2: equipment must be stretched; after stretching and before moving, check that it is coupled and for applied hand brakes that may normally be expected.

title: Rule 113.3
plain: Air must be used when switching on a grade greater than 0.4% AND with more than 2000 tons.

title: Rule 113.4
plain: Rule 113.4 prohibits them on main track, subdivision track, a siding, high-risk locations, shop tracks, and onto/with passenger equipment.

title: Rule 113.5
plain: Rule 113.5(a): on tracks not listed in Rule 113.4, unless otherwise indicated in special instructions, the kicking of equipment is prohibited.

title: Rule 113.6
plain: Rule 113.6(a): it must be verified that the switch and hand brakes are in working order before the move is commenced.

title: Rule 114
plain: Rule 114(b): a movement must not foul a track until the switches connected with the move are properly lined (or, for semi-automatic/spring switches, the conflicting route is known to be clear).

title: Rule 115
plain: Rule 115 needs a crew member positioned to observe and control the shove — EXCEPT where the track is known to be clear. 'Known to be clear' is a high bar, not a convenience.

title: Rule 120
plain: Rule 120(a): “OVER” = transmission is ended and a response is expected.

title: Rule 121
plain: The initiator ends the initial call with “OVER”; each party ends its FINAL transmission with “OUT” (121(c)).

title: Rule 122
plain: Rule 122: keep it brief and to the point, containing only essential instructions or information.

title: Rule 123
plain: Verification (Rule 123): safety-affecting radio instructions must be repeated back to the sender — this is why the engineer repeats each car/distance count.

title: Rule 123.2
plain: Rule 123.2(v): if there is doubt as to meaning or that the communication is for your movement, stop. Doubt is always a stop, radio or hand signal.

title: Rule 131
plain: Rule 131: the RTC maintains a complete record (book or computer-assisted system) made prior to or during the transmission.

title: Rule 132
plain: Rule 132: brief but clear, in the prescribed form, without erasure or any condition that may render it difficult to read.

title: Rule 133
plain: Rule 133: separate series per desk (except where computer-controlled) keeps numbering from colliding between desks and adjacent territories.

title: Rule 134
plain: Rule 134 (Designation of Movements): authorities are addressed clearly and concisely to those who must execute and observe them.

title: Rule 135
plain: Rule 135: the crew member copying a GBO/clearance must ensure those addressed receive a copy.

title: Rule 136
plain: Rule 136(a): copy as transmitted and repeat back from the copy received all applicable written/pre-printed portions, spelling out station names.

title: Rule 142
plain: Rule 142: read and have a proper understanding of all GBO/clearances as soon as possible, and make them available to other crew members as soon as practicable.

title: Rule 143
plain: Rule 143: GBO numbers applicable to a movement are shown on its clearance, so the crew knows which bulletins govern.

title: Rule 151
plain: Rule 151 (Identical Meaning to All): the body of each GBO must be given in the same words and figures to everyone addressed.

title: Rule 152
plain: Rule 152 (Delivery of GBO): the RTC must ensure affected movements receive a copy or are otherwise secured.

title: Rule 153
plain: Rule 153: don't confirm protection to a foreman until all affected movements have a copy of the GBO or are otherwise secured — that's what makes the limits safe.

title: Rule 156
plain: Rule 156: don't move where a DOB is applicable unless in possession of the current DOB (or a TGBO applicable within those limits).

title: Rule 157
plain: Rule 157: don't move where a TGBO is applicable unless in possession of a TGBO addressed to the movement.

title: Rule 301
plain: Rule 301: the RTC supervises OCS territory by means of clearances, TOP, GBO and other instructions as required.

title: Rule 302
plain: Rule 302(b): the clearance is sent direct to the crew; before acting on it the conductor and locomotive engineer must both confirm/understand it.

title: Rule 303
plain: Rule 303: where specified, protection against following trains/transfers must be provided before the RTC authorizes a movement to follow a preceding one.

title: Rule 304
plain: Rule 304: do not leave the point named until it is positively known the opposing train(s)/transfer(s) named on the clearance have arrived.

title: Rule 304.1
plain: Rule 304.1: stop clear of the route to be used by another train or transfer (stopping clear of the fouling point).

title: Rule 305
plain: Rule 305: before issuing clearance authority, the RTC must provide protection against all conflicting movements.

title: Rule 306
plain: Rule 306: in multi-track OCS, a clearance must specify the track(s) to be used.

title: Rule 308
plain: Rule 308(b): a work clearance remains in effect until superseded or cancelled, and permits movement in either direction within the named limits.

title: Rule 309
plain: Rule 309: you must protect against the named work — don't enter or move within its limits without the required authority/contact.

title: Rule 311
plain: Rule 311 (OCS): a movement is restricted by its clearance to protect against a foreman's working limits — you don't enter without that protection.

title: Rule 403
plain: A DV (diverging) plate upgrades the indication; remove it and the same lamp aspect downgrades to the Slow family.

title: Rule 405
plain: Clear (405): Proceed. A single green — two greens is Clear to Medium (407), not Clear.

title: Rule 411
plain: Clear to Stop (411): proceed, preparing to stop at the next signal — your block is clear, the next is not.

title: Rule 415
plain: The flash adds a block of advance warning: 415 prepares you to stop at the second signal, not the first.

title: Rule 416
plain: Flashing green = LIMITED; steady green = Medium/Clear. The flash is the tell that separates the Limited family from the Medium family.

title: Rule 430
plain: The DV plate is the upgrade: it raises Slow to Diverging. Remove it and the aspect downgrades to the Slow family.

title: Rule 436
plain: Two staggered reds with an R plate = Restricting (436); the same two staggered reds with no plate = Stop and Proceed (437).

title: Rule 437
plain: Two staggered reds with no plate = a non-controlled (automatic) signal: Stop and Proceed (437) — stop, then proceed at restricted speed.

title: Rule 439
plain: A single red on a controlled signal = Stop (439): an absolute stop with no proceeding (contrast the permissive two-staggered-reds of 437).

title: Rule 505
plain: Rule 505: block signals govern use of the blocks; they do not dispense with other signals and do not authorize main track occupancy.

title: Rule 507
plain: Rule 507: when ABS signals are withdrawn from service, movements are governed by RTC instructions or special instructions.

title: Rule 509
plain: Rule 509(a): a movement must have instructions from the RTC to pass a block signal indicating Stop.

title: Rule 513
plain: Rule 513(a): reverse the switch and wait five minutes (unless a greater period is specified), remaining at the switch, before moving foul of the main track.

title: Rule 515
plain: Rule 515: move at REDUCED speed to the next signal — unless there are no switches between, or until the track is seen to be clear.

title: Rule 560
plain: Rule 560: CTC is supervised by the RTC and block signals govern the operation of trains or transfers.

title: Rule 561
plain: Rule 561: when CTC is withdrawn from service, trains and transfers are governed by special instructions.

title: Rule 563
plain: Rule 563: when two opposing movements are lined into the same non-signalled siding, each locomotive engineer must be advised before the permitting signal is requested.

title: Rule 564
plain: A controlled signal is held at Stop by the RTC; you need authority (Rule 564) to pass it. An automatic block signal's red is permissive (stop and proceed).

title: Rule 565
plain: Rule 565: the movement is governed by Rule 564 within CTC and Rule 509 within ABS.

title: Rule 566
plain: Rule 566: work authority permits moving in either direction within the specified limits.

title: Rule 566.1
plain: Rule 566.1: such authority is included with work authority (566/567); the indications of those signals are suspended while switching.

title: Rule 567
plain: Rule 567 (joint work authority): each movement operates protecting against the other within the specified limits.

title: Rule 601
plain: Rule 601: within interlocking limits, interlocking signal indications govern the routes, and a signalman may issue instructions when necessary.

title: Rule 602
plain: Rule 602(a): except in emergency, radio or hand signals must not be used when the proper indication can be displayed by the interlocking signals.

title: Rule 604
plain: Rule 604(a): don't restore an approach signal to Stop unless the LE has acknowledged they're stopped or able to stop without passing the signal being restored.

title: Rule 605
plain: Rule 605: approach prepared to stop if occupying the timing circuit in excess of the time specified in special instructions.

title: Rule 606
plain: Rule 606: where there's no advance signal, approach interlocking limits prepared to comply with a signal indicating Stop.

title: Rule 607
plain: Rule 607: the applicable rule is set by the interlocking type (per special instructions) — 608/609/610/611.

title: Rule 608
plain: Rule 608: movements operating through the limits of a manual interlocking are governed by special instructions.

title: Rule 609
plain: Rule 609: a movement must have authority to pass a locally-controlled interlocking signal at Stop; the signalman may authorize it after the required checks.

title: Rule 610
plain: Rule 610: a movement must have authority; the signalman may authorize the pass only after ensuring no conflicting movement in the route.

title: Rule 611
plain: Rule 611: (a)/(b)/(c) apply when no other movement or track work is evident; (d) applies when track work IS evident.

title: Rule 612
plain: Rule 612: if you stop before fully passing a signal you'd accepted, you may proceed only after permission from the signalman or under the rule's provisions.

title: Rule 614
plain: Rule 614: when an interlocking is in ABS or CTC, the last interlocking signal's indication also governs the movement to the next signal or Block End sign.

title: Rule 615
plain: Rule 615: don't leave a single unit of equipment standing on a movable drawbridge portion or within the interlocking limits of a railway crossing at grade.

title: Rule 616
plain: Rule 616 (Damage to Interlocking) governs the response when a derailment or damage to the interlocking is known or suspected.

title: Rule 620
plain: Rule 620(b): operation beyond the governing stop sign must not be made until it's ascertained the route is properly lined (special instructions govern when an attendant is in charge).

title: Rule 849
plain: A TOP (Track Occupancy Permit) is the authority a foreman holds for the protection of track work and operation of track units.

title: General Rule A
plain: General Rule A(xi): sleeping or assuming the position of sleeping is prohibited, as is the use of personal entertainment devices.

title: General Rule B
plain: General Rule B: special instructions appear in timetables, general operating instructions, operating bulletins, or GBO.

title: General Rule C
plain: General Rule C(i): be vigilant to avoid the risk of injury to yourself or others.
